meta.name: Wave CSS Knowledge Base
meta.description: Structured documentation for Wave CSS web component library

overview.description: Wave CSS is a web component library using native Custom Elements without Shadow DOM. This allows seamless integration with third-party libraries and frameworks.

architecture.noShadowDOM: Components use regular DOM with classList.add('contents') for CSS containment
architecture.inheritance: All components extend WcBaseComponent or WcBaseFormComponent
architecture.cssVariables: Extensive theming system using CSS custom properties
architecture.htmxIntegration: Most components support HTMX attributes for server-driven interactions

lifecycle: Pending attributes are stored until component is ready
Child components are detected and awaited before initialization completes

buildOutput.versionedDist: dist/wave-css-0.0.1/ — auto-synced copy including icon bundles; also copied to go-kart static/js/wave-css-0.0.1/
buildOutput.cssGridUtilities: CSS grid utilities added at all breakpoints: grid-cols-1 through grid-cols-12, grid-rows, col-span, row-span, grid-flow

helperFunctions.loadScript: Concurrent callers now share the same promise via Map to prevent duplicate script loads and race conditions

layout.name: wc-sidenav
layout.description: Animated toggle-able side navigation with overlay, push, and open button
layout.usage: About Services Contact

layout.name: wc-split-pane
layout.description: Resizable split panel with draggable divider, horizontal/vertical layouts, collapsible panes, localStorage persistence, keyboard/touch support, and nesting
methods: toggle() — collapse or expand the start pane
layout.notes: direction='horizontal' (default, left/right) or 'vertical' (top/bottom). persist-key saves size/collapsed state to localStorage under 'wc-split-pane:{key}'. Keyboard: arrow keys resize (shift=50px step), Home/End min/max, Enter toggles collapse. Touch drag supported. Nestable for complex layouts.
layout.usage:
<wc-split-pane initial-size="256px" min-size="200px" max-size="400px" collapsible persist-key="sidebar">
  <wc-split-start>Left panel</wc-split-start>
  <wc-split-end>Right panel</wc-split-end>
</wc-split-pane>

navigation.name: wc-menu
navigation.description: Responsive navigation menu with HTMX support and mobile hamburger
navigation.notes: Uses declarative <option> children. Creates HTMX-powered links with hx-get, hx-target, hx-push-url.

navigation.name: wc-breadcrumb
navigation.description: Breadcrumb navigation with HTMX routing and document title management
navigation.usage:
<wc-breadcrumb doc-title="My App">
  <wc-breadcrumb-item label="Home" link="/"></wc-breadcrumb-item>
  <wc-breadcrumb-item label="Products" link="/products"></wc-breadcrumb-item>
  <wc-breadcrumb-item label="Details"></wc-breadcrumb-item>
</wc-breadcrumb>

navigation.name: wc-breadcrumb-item
navigation.description: Individual breadcrumb item (semantic wrapper read by wc-breadcrumb parent)

navigation.name: wc-tab
navigation.description: Tabbed interface with URL hash state, nested tab support, contrast control, removable tabs with context menu
navigation.notes: Active tab stored in URL hash (e.g., #Tab1+NestedTab). no-hash attribute suppresses URL hash updates on tab click (default behavior unchanged for backwards compatibility). Supports nested tabs with automatic contrast adjustment. tab-overflow attribute controls how tabs behave when they exceed available width: "scroll" (horizontal scroll, hidden scrollbar), "wrap" (tabs wrap to multiple lines), "scroll-wrap" (wrap with scroll fallback). removable attribute adds close buttons on dynamically added tabs and enables right-click context menu (Close, Close Others, Close to Right, Close to Left, Close All) powered by wc-context-menu.
navigation.usage:
London

Content...

New York

Content...

 ... ... ... ... ...

navigation.name: wc-tab-item
navigation.usage: Tab content here

navigation.name: wc-dropdown
navigation.description: Dropdown menu with CSS Anchor Positioning, search mode, and mobile fallback
navigation.notes: Format options: default button, 'grid-round' (hamburger), 'avatar'. Mode 'search' adds filter input. btn-class attribute styles the trigger button.

navigation.name: wc-tree
navigation.description: Hierarchical tree component with nested items, expand/collapse, icons, badges, search, lazy loading, keyboard navigation, and HTMX support
navigation.notes: Supports nested wc-tree-item children. Search attribute enables a filter input. lazy-url enables on-demand child loading. Full keyboard navigation (arrow keys, enter, space). HTMX attributes supported for server-driven interactions.
navigation.usage:
<wc-tree search>
  <wc-tree-item label="Documents" icon="fa-folder" expanded>
    <wc-tree-item label="Resume.pdf" icon="fa-file"></wc-tree-item>
    <wc-tree-item label="Cover Letter.docx" icon="fa-file"></wc-tree-item>
  </wc-tree-item>
  <wc-tree-item label="Photos" icon="fa-folder" badge="12">
    <wc-tree-item label="Vacation" icon="fa-folder" lazy-url="/api/photos/vacation"></wc-tree-item>
  </wc-tree-item>
</wc-tree>

navigation.name: wc-tree-item
navigation.description: Tree node used as a child of wc-tree or another wc-tree-item for nesting. Supports hover-reveal action buttons, HTMX attributes, and expand/collapse events.
navigation.notes: Click behavior: only the arrow toggle expands/collapses; clicking the label selects. data-tree-action child elements render as hover-reveal action buttons. contextmenu event is prevented. htmx.process is called with guard check after render.
navigation.usage:
<wc-tree-item label="Node" icon="fa-folder" badge="3" expanded>
  <wc-tree-item label="Child" icon="fa-file" icon-style="color:red;"></wc-tree-item>
</wc-tree-item>

<!-- With hover action buttons -->
<wc-tree-item label="Document" icon="fa-file">
  <button data-tree-action onclick="editDoc()">Edit</button>
  <button data-tree-action onclick="deleteDoc()">Delete</button>
</wc-tree-item>

form.name: wc-form
form.description: Form wrapper that manages submission and temporarily enables disabled fields during submit
form.usage:
<wc-form id="myForm" method="POST" action="/submit">
  <wc-input name="username" lbl-label="Username" required></wc-input>
  <wc-textarea name="notes" lbl-label="Notes" rows="4"></wc-textarea>
  <button type="submit" class="btn btn-primary">Submit</button>
</wc-form>

form.name: wc-input
form.description: Text input supporting text, email, password, tel, search, number, date, currency, checkbox, radio, and toggle switch
form.notes: Radio buttons use <option> children. Toggle switch creates visual toggle UI. Password has visibility toggle. Currency/email/tel/search have built-in icons.

usage.text: <wc-input name="email" type="email" lbl-label="Email" required placeholder="Enter email"></wc-input>
usage.radio:
<wc-input name="gender" type="radio" lbl-label="Gender" value="male" auto-flex>
  <option value="male">Male</option>
  <option value="female">Female</option>
</wc-input>
usage.tooltip: <wc-input name="code" type="text" lbl-label="Code" tooltip="Enter 6-digit code" tooltip-position="right"></wc-input>

form.name: wc-select
form.description: Dropdown select with chip mode for multi-select, URL-based data loading, and keyboard navigation
form.notes: Supports standard mode and chip mode. URL loading fetches JSON with optional results-member, display-member, value-member. Supports optgroups. allow-dynamic lets users add custom values. Value getter proxies to inner <select> element. Change events dispatch with bubbles:true from the outer wc-select element. Chip mode change events also bubble. optionsloaded event fires after URL-based options are populated and value is applied. Detail: { value, optionCount }. Hyperscript-compatible (no colons).

usage.url:
<wc-select name="dept" lbl-label="Department"
  url="/api/departments"
  display-member="name"
  value-member="id"
  sort="asc">
</wc-select>
usage.optgroups:
<wc-select name="fruit" lbl-label="Fruit">
  <optgroup label="Citrus">
    <option value="orange">Orange</option>
    <option value="lemon">Lemon</option>
  </optgroup>
  <optgroup label="Berries">
    <option value="blueberry">Blueberry</option>
  </optgroup>
</wc-select>

form.name: wc-textarea
form.description: Multi-line text input with optional emoji shortcode autocomplete
form.notes: With emoji-shortcodes attribute, typing ':' triggers autocomplete popup for emoji insertion.
form.usage: <wc-textarea name="message" lbl-label="Message" rows="5" emoji-shortcodes placeholder="Type :smile: for emoji"></wc-textarea>

form.name: wc-field
form.description: Read-only display field with optional clickable label link and HTMX support
form.notes: NOT a form element. Display-only. Child elements take precedence over value attribute. Label can be a link via 'link' attribute or HTMX attributes.

display.name: wc-accordion
display.description: Collapsible content sections supporting single or multiple open panels
display.notes: Data via items JSON attribute or declarative <option> elements. HTMX support.
display.usage:
<wc-accordion allow-many>
  <option value="Section 1" selected>Content for section 1...</option>
  <option value="Section 2">Content for section 2...</option>
</wc-accordion>

display.name: wc-article-card
display.description: Article card that fetches metadata from a URL endpoint
display.notes: Fetches from /api/article-metadata?url=... Returns title, description, imageUrl, publishDate, domain.

display.name: wc-contact-card
display.description: Contact information card with avatar image

display.name: wc-contact-chip
display.description: Compact inline contact display with close button

display.name: wc-flip-box
display.description: 3D flippable content box with hover or click activation
display.notes: Uses slot='front' and slot='back' for content. flip-on='click' adds flip buttons; default is hover.
display.usage:
Front

Back

display.name: wc-google-map
display.description: Google Maps integration with single/multiple pins and info windows
display.notes: Multiple pins via <option data-lat data-lng data-title> children. Uses AdvancedMarkerElement.

usage.multiple:
<wc-google-map api-key="YOUR_KEY" zoom="10">
  <option data-lat="40.7128" data-lng="-74.0060" data-title="New York"></option>
  <option data-lat="34.0522" data-lng="-118.2437" data-title="Los Angeles"></option>
</wc-google-map>

display.name: wc-image
display.description: Enhanced image with optional modal overlay and hover effects
display.notes: hover-mode options: top, bottom, left, right. Modal creates full-screen overlay on click.

display.name: wc-background-image
display.description: Background image container with parallax effect and caption

display.name: wc-timeline
display.description: Vertical timeline with alternating left/right cards
display.notes: Data via items JSON or declarative <option> children. Cards alternate left/right with connecting arrows.
display.usage:
<wc-timeline>
  <option value="2024">Founded the company...</option>
  <option value="2025">Launched product v2...</option>
  <option value="2026">Expanded globally...</option>
</wc-timeline>

display.name: wc-slideshow
display.description: Image slideshow with prev/next navigation and autoplay
display.usage:
<wc-slideshow autoplay autoplay-interval="3000">
  <wc-slideshow-image url="image1.jpg" caption="Slide 1"></wc-slideshow-image>
  <wc-slideshow-image url="image2.jpg" caption="Slide 2"></wc-slideshow-image>
</wc-slideshow>

data.name: wc-tabulator
data.description: Advanced data table powered by Tabulator.js with column definitions, row menus, pagination, auto-columns, and HTMX sync
data.notes: Column definitions via <wc-tabulator-column> children. Custom functions via <wc-tabulator-func>. Row context menus via <wc-tabulator-row-menu>. Supports pagination, column resizing, movable columns, selectable rows, filters. auto-columns attribute with data generates columns automatically from data keys: flattens nested objects with dot notation, detects types (string/number/boolean/date/ObjectID/array), freezes _id column first, makes columns sortable/resizable/reorderable. Reactive to data attribute updates.
data.usage:
<wc-tabulator id="myTable">
  <wc-tabulator-column title="Name" field="name" editor="input"></wc-tabulator-column>
  <wc-tabulator-column title="Email" field="email"></wc-tabulator-column>
  <wc-tabulator-column title="Age" field="age" sorter="number"></wc-tabulator-column>
</wc-tabulator>

data.name: wc-table
data.description: Lightweight data-driven table with sorting, row click events, auto-columns, and URL-based data loading
data.notes: Column definitions via <wc-table-col> children. auto-columns generates columns from data keys. url attribute fetches JSON data. items attribute accepts JSON array. clickable enables row click/dblclick events.
data.usage:
<wc-table url="/api/users" striped hoverable clickable>
  <wc-table-col field="name" label="Name" sortable></wc-table-col>
  <wc-table-col field="email" label="Email" sortable></wc-table-col>
  <wc-table-col field="role" label="Role" align="center" width="120px"></wc-table-col>
</wc-table>

data.name: wc-table-col
data.description: Column definition for wc-table specifying field mapping, label, sorting, alignment, width, and formatting
data.usage: <wc-table-col field="name" label="Full Name" sortable align="left" width="200px"></wc-table-col>

data.name: wc-chart-builder
data.description: Interactive chart component that renders charts from arbitrary JSON data with auto-detection and field picker UI
data.notes: chart-type options: bar, line, pie, doughnut, area, number. auto-detect analyzes data to choose chart type, label field, and value fields automatically. show-controls displays a configuration bar with Type, Label, and Values dropdowns for user-driven customization. colors attribute accepts a JSON array of hex colors. Host element uses display: contents (via super._render()) for proper flex layout participation. maintainAspectRatio defaults to true when no explicit height is set; false when height attribute is provided. No height attribute = Chart.js default aspect ratio; explicit height = fills the container.
data.usage:
<wc-chart-builder data='[{"name":"Alice","sales":100},{"name":"Bob","sales":200}]' auto-detect show-controls title="Sales by Rep"></wc-chart-builder>

<!-- Explicit configuration -->
<wc-chart-builder data='[...]' chart-type="pie" label-field="name" value-fields="sales" title="Sales Distribution"></wc-chart-builder>

data.name: wc-pivot
data.description: Full-featured cross-tabulation pivot table with field panel, drag-to-zone assignment, value filters, drill-down detail rows, date grouping, config save/load, compact layout, heatmap, sorting, and CSV export
data.notes: aggregate options: count, sum, avg, min, max. number-format options: integer, decimal, currency, percent. auto-detect selects row-field, col-field, and value-field from data. show-controls displays configuration dropdowns. show-heatmap applies color intensity to cells based on value. Sticky headers and row labels. Column headers are sortable. sort-rows options: asc, desc, value-asc, value-desc. max-columns limits the number of column values displayed. Phase 1 — Field Panel: show-field-panel attribute or hamburger button toggles a sidebar listing all data fields with type badges (Abc for string, # for number, T/F for boolean, calendar for date). Four drop zones: Filters, Rows, Columns, Values. Click a field to open a popover for zone assignment. Zone chips show filter icon and remove button. Value filter dropdown with checkbox list, search, All/None/Apply. Active filters highlighted with yellow outline. Values zone chips display 'Sum of Revenue' style labels; click to change aggregate via popover. Phase 2 — Drill-Down: click any pivot cell to expand an inline detail table showing matching source documents with smooth slide animation. Expand icon appears on cell hover. Detail table shows all flattened fields with formatted dates. Click same cell or close button to collapse. pivot:drill-down event includes { row, column, expanded, documents }. Phase 3 — Config & Layout: getConfig() returns full pivot state as JSON (zones, filters, dateGrouping, sort, layout, formatting). loadConfig(json) restores from config object or JSON string. config attribute for declarative pre-configuration. compact-layout attribute or toggle button reduces padding and font size for dense display. Date fields auto-detected with calendar badge. Date grouping options: year, quarter, month, year-month, day, datetime. Auto-defaults to month grouping when date field placed in rows or columns. Calendar icon on date chips opens grouping popover. Dates in drill-down detail formatted as human-readable (e.g. Jan 15, 2025 10:00 AM). Host element uses display: contents (via super._render()) for proper flex layout participation.
data.usage:
<wc-pivot data='[{"region":"East","product":"Widget","sales":100},...]' auto-detect show-controls show-totals show-heatmap show-field-panel></wc-pivot>

<!-- Explicit configuration -->
<wc-pivot data='[...]' row-field="region" col-field="product" value-field="sales" aggregate="sum" number-format="currency" show-totals></wc-pivot>

<!-- With saved config -->
<wc-pivot data='[...]' config='{"zones":{"rows":["region"],"columns":["product"],"values":[{"field":"sales","aggregate":"sum"}]},"formatting":{"numberFormat":"currency","showTotals":true}}' show-controls></wc-pivot>

<!-- Compact layout with date grouping -->
<wc-pivot data='[...]' auto-detect compact-layout show-totals show-field-panel></wc-pivot>
demoViews: views/pivot.html — Field Panel demo, auto-detect count, currency sum, boolean column, quarterly sales, date grouping, datetime server logs, config save/load, dynamic data, drill-down with cell click events

data.name: wc-explain-tree
data.description: Visual MongoDB explain plan viewer, renders stage-by-stage flow diagram
data.notes: Summary bar with green/yellow/red status indicators. Stage nodes with color-coded types: COLLSCAN=red, IXSCAN=green, FETCH=blue, SORT=yellow. Bottom-to-top flow with connectors. Branching for $or queries. Aggregation pipeline support. Click-to-expand raw JSON detail (inline). Loading indicator. data property setter accepts object or JSON string. Unwraps Go Kart { raw, summary } wrapper. Host element: wc-explain-tree { display: contents; }.

interactive.name: wc-context-menu
interactive.description: Reusable context menu with singleton pattern, declarative or programmatic items, auto-close on click outside or Escape
childElements: wc-context-menu-item — declarative menu item with label, icon, action, disabled, divider attributes
staticMethods: WcContextMenu.show(x, y, items) — show singleton context menu at position with items array
WcContextMenu.hide() — hide the singleton context menu
methods: open(x, y, items) — show this instance at position (reads declarative children if items omitted)
interactive.notes: Only one context menu visible at a time (singleton pattern for static API). Items support: label, icon (wc-fa-icon name), action (function), disabled, divider. Used by wc-tab for removable tab context menus. Auto-positions to stay within viewport bounds.

usage.declarative:
<wc-context-menu id="my-menu">
  <wc-context-menu-item label="Query" icon="magnifying-glass" action="doQuery()"></wc-context-menu-item>
  <wc-context-menu-item divider></wc-context-menu-item>
  <wc-context-menu-item label="Delete" icon="trash" action="doDelete()" disabled></wc-context-menu-item>
</wc-context-menu>

interactive.name: wc-code-mirror
interactive.description: Code editor integration using CodeMirror with dynamic themes, modes, settings popover, and autocomplete hints
interactive.notes: Form-associated custom element. Supports dynamic theme/mode loading. Settings popover for runtime configuration. fetch attribute loads code from URL. DependencyManager loads CodeMirror 5.65.13. hint-words (JSON array) and hint-url (URL returning JSON array) enable Ctrl+Space autocomplete. Auto-wraps completions in double quotes when inside JSON context (detects { } braces). Hint addon loaded only when hint attributes present. hint-url skips fetch when empty/blank (guard added). .CodeMirror-hints z-index set to 100000 so hints render above modals. JSON context detection (_isJsonContext) now scans backward across multiple lines, not just the current line — fixes auto-quote wrapping in multi-line JSON like {\n  \n}. Mode scripts are now pre-loaded BEFORE editor creation via _preloadMode() so syntax highlighting works on first render in HTMX-loaded fragments. Editor is always created (never deferred/null) — this.editor is always available after connectedCallback. One-time mode re-apply on first change event fixes syntax highlighting for editors created in hidden containers. display() method forces re-tokenization + refresh (call after making editor visible). value property setter re-applies mode after setValue for hidden-container fix. Pongo2/Django template syntax highlighting via overlay ({% %} tags in violet, {{ }} variables in coral, {# #} comments in gray). .cm-error red background suppressed globally. Additional language modes supported: python, go, ruby, rust, sql, shell, yaml, toml, dockerfile, swift, and clike-based MIME types (text/x-java, text/x-csharp, etc.).
interactive.usage:
<wc-code-mirror name="code" line-numbers mode="javascript" theme="monokai" tab-size="2">
function hello() {
  return 'world';
}
</wc-code-mirror>

interactive.name: wc-ai-bot
interactive.description: AI chatbot interface using WebLLM (MLC AI) for in-browser LLM inference
interactive.notes: Runs LLMs in browser via WebGPU. Auto-detects GPU capability. Supports streaming responses. Markdown rendering via marked library.
interactive.usage: <wc-ai-bot model="Llama-3.2-3B-Instruct-q4f16_1-MLC" system-prompt="You are a helpful assistant." title="AI Assistant" theme="sidebar"></wc-ai-bot>

interactive.name: wc-hf-bot
interactive.description: AI chatbot using Transformers.js (Hugging Face) for browser-based inference
interactive.notes: Same UI and API as wc-ai-bot but uses Hugging Face Transformers.js with ONNX/GGUF models.
interactive.usage: <wc-hf-bot model="Xenova/distilgpt2" system-prompt="You are helpful." title="HF Assistant" theme="bubble"></wc-hf-bot>

buttons.name: wc-save-button
buttons.description: Save button with form validation that triggers HTMX 'validated' event
buttons.notes: Validates form including hidden fields in accordions/tabs before triggering save.

buttons.name: wc-save-split-button
buttons.description: Split button with Save, Save & New, and Save & Return options
buttons.notes: Handles HTMX redirect headers. save-url, save-new-url, save-return-url for each action. btn-class attribute styles the save button.

buttons.name: wc-split-button
buttons.description: Generic split button with dropdown for additional actions
buttons.notes: btn-class attribute is now wired up (was declared but unused previously).

skeleton.name: wc-loader
skeleton.description: Loading spinner with configurable size, speed, and thickness

utility.name: wc-event-hub
utility.description: Global event broadcasting system. Singleton at window.wc.EventHub
utility.notes: Central pub/sub for inter-component communication. Events dispatched on document.body and parent window.

utility.name: wc-event-handler
utility.description: Declarative event handler that responds to custom events with DOM actions
utility.notes: Actions: add-class, remove-class, toggle-class, add-item, remove-item, click

utility.name: wc-notify
utility.description: Toast notification system with stacking, auto-dismiss, and persistent options

utility.name: wc-prompt
utility.description: Dialog system using SweetAlert2 and notie for banners, toasts, and modal dialogs. Simple dialog methods use focusConfirm:false, backdrop:false, scrollbarPadding:false to prevent scroll reset.
utility.usage:
wc.Prompt.success({ title: 'Done!', text: 'Record saved' });
wc.Prompt.question({ title: 'Confirm?', text: 'Delete this record?', callback: (result) => {} });

utility.name: wc-theme-selector
utility.description: Interactive theme switcher with 40+ theme color palette and light/dark mode toggle

utility.name: wc-hotkey
utility.description: Keyboard shortcut binding that triggers click or custom action on target element
utility.notes: Parses key combinations like ctrl+s, alt+n. Triggers click on target selector by default. The action attribute specifies a custom action instead of click.

utility.name: wc-link
utility.description: Dynamic CSS stylesheet loader. Prevents duplicates. Removes self after loading.

utility.name: wc-script
utility.description: Dynamic JavaScript loader. Prevents duplicates. Removes self after loading.

utility.name: wc-javascript
utility.description: Inline JavaScript executor. Moves content to <script> tag in head.

utility.name: wc-behavior
utility.description: HTMX attribute attachment - copies HTMX attributes to parent element

utility.name: wc-mask-hub
utility.description: Input masking system using IMask library for phone, SSN, ZIP, date, currency formats

utility.name: wc-visibility-change
utility.description: Fires HTMX request when browser tab becomes visible (e.g., for auto-refresh)

designer.name: wc-live-designer
designer.description: Visual page builder. Server-rendered iframe shows production-accurate preview. Source tab shows full Pongo2 template. Property panel reads from rendered DOM. sourceDoc (DOMParser Document) is source of truth for save.

architecture.iframe: Loads server-rendered page with ?designer=true. Go Kart adds data-designer to <html>.
architecture.designerBridge: designer-bridge.js auto-initializes when html[data-designer] is detected. Handles selection, drag/drop, toolbar, postMessage.
architecture.designerCSS: Scoped under html[data-designer] in wave-css.css. Selection outlines, drop targets, skeleton indicators, interaction blocking.
architecture.sourceDoc: DOMParser Document maintained by parent. Source of truth for save. data-designer-id attributes persist across save/reload.
architecture.propertyPanel: Reads from rendered DOM via bridge postMessage. Shows Pongo2 expressions for value bindings.
architecture.sourceTab: Full Pongo2 template with {% extends %}, {% block %}, {{ Record.field }}, data-designer-id.

presets: Standard Edit Page — skeleton + nav + tabs + form + change log
Simple Form Page — skeleton + nav + form (no tabs)
Name Fields / Address Block / Contact Fields patterns

themes.description: 40+ predefined themes using oklch color space with automatic light/dark mode support
themes.usage: Apply theme class to <html> element or use <wc-theme> component
themes.darkMode: Add 'dark' class alongside theme class, or use @media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)

cssUtilities.description: 3,700+ Tailwind-inspired utility classes with responsive breakpoints and pseudo-class variants

spacing.margin: m-*, mx-*, my-*, mt-*, mr-*, mb-*, ml-* (0 to 64, also auto and negative)

sizing.width: w-0 to w-64, w-full, w-screen, w-auto, w-1/2, w-1/3, w-2/3, w-3/4
sizing.height: h-0 to h-64, h-full, h-screen, h-auto

cssUtilities.responsiveUsage: Prefix any utility with breakpoint: sm:flex, md:grid-2, lg:text-lg, xl:w-1/2
cssUtilities.pseudoClassUsage: Prefix with state: hover:bg-blue-500, focus:border-primary, active:scale-95

eventHub.description: Central pub/sub system for inter-component communication

patterns.simplePageTemplate: Hello Wave CSS!
patterns.dataTableTemplate:
<wc-tabulator id="contactsTable">
  <wc-tabulator-column title="Name" field="name" editor="input" sorter="string"></wc-tabulator-column>
  <wc-tabulator-column title="Email" field="email" sorter="string"></wc-tabulator-column>
  <wc-tabulator-column title="Status" field="status" editor="select" editor-params='{"values":["Active","Inactive"]}'></wc-tabulator-column>
</wc-tabulator>

viewFiles.description: Example HTML files in views/ directory demonstrating components
keyViews: views/chart-builder.html — Chart Builder demos (bar, line, pie, doughnut, area, number charts with auto-detect and controls)
views/pivot.html — Pivot Table demos (cross-tabulation with heatmap, sorting, CSV export)
views/tab.html — Tab component with dynamic tabs and context menu testing
views/index.html — Main navigation hub using HTMX (includes chart-builder and pivot links)

routing.description: Template-based routing used with Go Kart framework
